name: TLBX
description: 2HP precision utility for multing, mixing and differencing audio or CV. Generates 1V and 5V references, normalizes sections together, and doubles as a range converter, octave transposer and comparator.

inputs.name: MIX IN 2
inputs.description: Secondary mixer input, normalized to MIX IN 1 when unpatched.

outputs.name: MULT OUT 1
outputs.description: Precision mult output and 1V reference when nothing is patched.

outputs.name: MULT OUT 2
outputs.description: Precision mult output and 5V reference when nothing is patched.

outputs.name: MIX OUT
outputs.description: Precision summed output from the two mixer inputs.

outputs.name: DIFF SUM
outputs.description: Adder output from the diff section.

outputs.name: DIFF DIFF
outputs.description: Subtractor output from the diff section.

behaviors.name: Reference source
behaviors.description: The mult section turns into a calibrated 1V / 5V reference pair for pitch transposition and voltage checking.

behaviors.name: Normalized mix
behaviors.trigger: patch one signal into MIX IN 1
behaviors.description: MIX IN 2 follows MIX IN 1 when left open, turning the section into a precision 2x multiplier or small signal adder.

behaviors.name: Adder / subtractor
behaviors.description: The diff section exposes summed and difference outputs, which makes it useful for stereo-to-mono, bipolar offsets and phase comparison.

behaviors.name: Voltage range conversion
behaviors.description: The normalization between sections lets TLBX translate between 0-5V, -5-5V and 0-10V signal ranges without a full utility row.

behaviors.name: Comparator-like behavior
behaviors.trigger: run CV through the diff and sum paths
behaviors.description: The precision structure and fixed references make it easy to derive threshold-style results or octave-locked pitch offsets.

patchIdeas.name: Reference calibrator
patchIdeas.patch: Leave MULT unpatched · use 1V and 5V refs to calibrate oscillators, VCA trims or sequencer offsets · the tiny module becomes a bench reference tucked into the rack.
patchIdeas.diagram:
TLBX:

VCO:
VCA:

- TLBX (MULT OUT 1) p> VCO (V/Oct)
- TLBX (MULT OUT 2) >> VCA (CV)
- VCO (Out) -> Output (In)

patchIdeas.name: Octave doubler
patchIdeas.patch: Feed a sequencer row into MIX IN 1 and leave MIX IN 2 normalized · take the MIX output as a precise 2x version of the line for octave jumps or range expansion.
patchIdeas.diagram:
TLBX:

Sequencer:
VCO:
Output:

- Sequencer (Pitch) p> TLBX (MIX IN 1)
- TLBX (MIX OUT) p> VCO (V/Oct)
- VCO (Out) -> Output (In)

patchIdeas.name: Stereo gain trim
patchIdeas.patch: Use DIFF as a precision mono-to-stereo helper · sum one source and subtract another · the module acts as a tiny matrix for balancing two adjacent voices.
patchIdeas.diagram:
TLBX:

Voice A:
Voice B:
Mixer:
Output:

- Voice A (Out) -> TLBX (DIFF IN 1)
- Voice B (Out) -> TLBX (DIFF IN 2)
- TLBX (DIFF SUM) -> Mixer (L)
- TLBX (DIFF DIFF) -> Mixer (R)
- Mixer (Out) -> Output (In)

patchIdeas.name: Range shifter
patchIdeas.patch: Use the three sections as a chain · mult a pitch line, mix in an offset and subtract the result against a second lane · the patch moves between 0-5V, bipolar and unipolar control ranges cleanly.
patchIdeas.diagram:
TLBX:

Sequencer:
Quantizer:
Output:

- Sequencer (Pitch) p> TLBX (MULT IN)
- TLBX (MULT OUT 1) p> Quantizer (In)
- Quantizer (Out) p> Output (In)
